title: Fording a Fast-Flowing River
immediate_steps: Scout for the widest, shallowest part of the river (avoid narrow, deep, fast sections)
Keep your boots on to protect your feet, but remove your pants to reduce drag
Unbuckle your backpack waist strap (so you can ditch it if you fall)
Find a sturdy stick to use as a third leg for balance
Face upstream and cross at a slight angle downstream
Shuffle sideways, maintaining two points of contact at all times
avoid: Do not cross if the water is above your knees and moving fast
Do not tie yourself to a rope stretched across the river (it can pull you under)
